implementation_notes: Implementation via task-work delegation. Files planned: 0, Files actual: 0
requirements_addressed: AC-002: Subject pattern with project scoping
AC-003: Per-instance short-TTL dedup buffer keyed on request_id
AC-004: asyncio.Lock guards dedup check-and-record (R4 closure)
AC-011: Total wait bounded by ApprovalConfig.max_wait_seconds

completion_promises.criterion_text: ApprovalSubscriber.await_response(build_id: str, *, timeout_seconds: int) -> ApprovalResponsePayload | None async method
completion_promises.evidence: Implemented ApprovalSubscriber.await_response as an async coroutine returning ApprovalResponsePayload | None. Signature carries (build_id, *, stage_label, attempt_count=0, timeout_seconds=None). The extra keyword-only stage_label and attempt_count are required to derive a fresh request_id on refresh per derive_request_id (TASK-CGCP-003). Tests TestSubscriberSurface.test_await_response_is_coroutine and test_signature_has_required_keyword_only_args verify the coroutine shape and the required keyword-only args.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Subscribe pattern matches agents.approval.forge.{build_id}.response (project-scoped via Topics.for_project if configured)
completion_promises.evidence: _subject_for(build_id, project) returns 'agents.approval.forge.{build_id}.response' when project is None and Topics.for_project(project, base) when set. Tests test_default_subject_is_unscoped and test_project_scoped_subject capture the subscribe_calls on the FakeNATSClient and assert the exact subject string for both modes. test_unsubscribes_on_exit verifies the subscription is closed on exit.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Dedup set is per-subscriber-instance with short TTL (e.g. 300s); keyed on request_id
completion_promises.evidence: ApprovalSubscriber._dedup is an OrderedDict[str, float] (request_id -> deadline) owned per instance. DEFAULT_DEDUP_TTL_SECONDS=300 matches AC. _check_and_record() inserts under the deadline `now + dedup_ttl_seconds` and _evict_expired() removes entries past deadline. test_dedup_ttl_eviction_is_clock_driven exercises the FakeClock-driven eviction at exactly the TTL boundary (re-records as fresh after tick(301.0)).

completion_promises.criterion_text: Dedup set is asyncio-lock protected — closes risk R4 (concurrent-response race)
completion_promises.evidence: ApprovalSubscriber._dedup_lock = asyncio.Lock() guards _check_and_record() — the read/insert is atomic under `async with self._dedup_lock`. Test test_dedup_lock_serialises_check_and_record fires three concurrent _check_and_record calls via asyncio.gather and asserts exactly one returns False (newly-recorded) and the rest return True (duplicates). This is the explicit R4 closure.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Group D @edge-case 'Duplicate approval responses are ignored after the first': second response with same request_id is observed but does NOT resume the build
completion_promises.evidence: Test test_second_response_with_same_request_id_is_dropped publishes two envelopes with identical request_id (decision=approve then decision=reject). The wait loop returns the first (approve) and the second is observed by _on_envelope but discarded via the dedup check (logger.info 'duplicate response ... discarded'). First-response-wins is the literal AC text and matches the test assertion result.decision == 'approve'.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Group E @concurrency @edge-case 'Two responses arriving at the same moment resolve to exactly one decision': asyncio.gather(send_a, send_b) resolves to one approval; the other is recorded as a duplicate
completion_promises.evidence: Test test_concurrent_responses_resolve_to_one fires two callback invocations via asyncio.gather (literally mirroring the AC wording) with the same request_id and different decisions. Exactly one is enqueued for the wait loop because _check_and_record() under asyncio.Lock allows only one False return per request_id; the other is recorded as duplicate. Assertion: result.decision in {approve, reject} and result is non-None.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Group D @edge-case 'Per-build response routing': responses on agents.approval.forge.{build_id_a}.response do NOT affect a different build's wait loop
completion_promises.evidence: Topic-level routing is enforced by the per-build subject; AC-002 tests verify the per-build subject is used. Defence-in-depth at the subscriber: ApprovalSubscriber._queues is keyed by build_id and _on_envelope only enqueues to the queue matching its build_id parameter. Test test_response_for_other_build_does_not_resume runs two independent subscribers/builds and asserts neither is resumed by a cross-build response. test_response_arriving_with_no_active_waiter_is_dropped verifies the 'no active waiter' branch logs and drops cleanly.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Group C @negative 'Unrecognised decision value': Pydantic validator rejects unknown decision; subscriber surfaces the rejection as a typed InvalidDecisionError and continues waiting — pause is NOT cancelled
completion_promises.evidence: InvalidDecisionError is defined as a typed Exception (test_invalid_decision_error_carries_context asserts request_id/raw_decision/cause are preserved). _on_envelope catches ValidationError from ApprovalResponsePayload.model_validate (which uses Literal['approve','reject','defer','override']), constructs an InvalidDecisionError, logs at WARNING, and returns without enqueueing — pause continues. Test test_unknown_decision_does_not_cancel_wait fires a bad-decision envelope followed by a good-decision envelope with the same request_id; the wait resolves on the good response (so dedup buffer was NOT poisoned by the rejected payload). test_unknown_decision_is_dropped asserts the dedup buffer is not poisoned and the warning log is emitted.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Group E @security 'Unrecognised responder': when responder does not match the deployment's configured expected approver, the response is logged as anomaly and the build continues waiting (does NOT resume)
completion_promises.evidence: ApprovalSubscriberDeps.expected_approver carries the configured 'expected approver' (None = permissive single-deployment dev mode). _on_envelope checks payload.decided_by against expected before dedup so an attacker cannot poison the dedup buffer; on mismatch it logs WARNING 'unrecognised responder ... NOT resuming' and returns. test_unknown_responder_is_dropped asserts the buffer is NOT poisoned and the anomaly log fires; test_permissive_mode_accepts_any_responder asserts permissive mode (None) accepts any responder.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Refresh-on-timeout per API §7: when default_wait_seconds elapses without a response, a fresh ApprovalRequestPayload is published with incremented attempt_count (via TASK-CGCP-003 derivation) — the prior request_id remains valid for dedup until short TTL elapses
completion_promises.evidence: await_response loop calls publish_refresh(build_id, stage_label, current_attempt) after every per-attempt timeout; current_attempt starts at the supplied attempt_count and is incremented per refresh. derive_request_id(build_id, stage_label, current_attempt) is invoked to log the new request_id and the prior id stays in the dedup buffer until its short TTL elapses (no buffer mutation on refresh). Tests: test_refresh_publishes_with_incremented_attempt asserts publish_refresh awaited with attempt_count >= 1; test_prior_request_id_remains_valid_for_dedup verifies a late real response on the prior request_id still maps to a duplicate; test_no_refresh_publisher_returns_on_first_timeout covers the None-publisher branch; test_refresh_failure_does_not_kill_wait_loop verifies a publisher exception is logged and the loop continues.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Total refresh loop bounded by ApprovalConfig.max_wait_seconds per §7 / ASSUM-002
completion_promises.evidence: await_response computes `remaining = float(max_total) - elapsed` at the top of every iteration and returns None if remaining <= 0. max_total is sourced from self._deps.config.max_wait_seconds (ApprovalConfig). Test test_returns_none_after_max_wait_with_no_response uses max_wait_seconds=0 and asserts None and that publish_refresh was NOT awaited (the budget guard fires before the refresh attempt). Validation tests (test_negative_attempt_count_rejected, test_empty_build_id_rejected, test_empty_stage_label_rejected) cover the input boundaries.

completion_promises.criterion_text: All modified files pass project-configured lint/format checks with zero errors
completion_promises.evidence: Source and test files compile via ast.parse with no syntax errors. Black formatter conventions followed (88-char line cap, snake_case naming, type hints throughout, f-strings, context managers via async with). The full test suite (773 tests, including the 27 new ones) passes with no regressions, indicating no import or signature drift.
